skill.name: resolving-merge-conflicts
skill.description: Guides agents through resolving in-progress Git merge or rebase conflicts by inspecting history, preserving intent, running checks, and completing the operation.
skill.summary: Resolve Git merge and rebase conflicts with source-aware, test-backed steps.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: The static external command finding is a false positive caused by inline Markdown around --abort in guidance text. The weak cryptographic algorithm finding is also a false positive; SKILL.md line 3 is plain YAML description text with no cryptographic operation. No prompt injection, network use, data exfiltration, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Inline Git Flag Marked as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution at SKILL.md line 10. The line contains Markdown inline code for the git flag --abort inside written guidance, not executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is Markdown documentation, and the repository contains only SKILL.md. There is no script, shell template, or executable command construction at this location.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Description Text Marked as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged a weak cryptographic algorithm at SKILL.md line 3. The line is the skill description and contains no cryptographic API, algorithm name, import, or executable logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is plain YAML front matter describing merge and rebase conflict handling. I found no evidence of cryptographic code or configuration in the scanned file.

content.value_statement: Merge and rebase conflicts can hide intent from both sides of a change. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through source-aware resolution, checks, and completion.
actual_capabilities: Checks the current Git merge or rebase state before editing conflicts.
Directs review of history, commit messages, pull requests, and issues.
Guides hunk-by-hunk resolution while preserving both change intents.
Requires automated project checks after conflict resolution.
Instructs the agent to stage changes and finish the merge or rebase.
limitations: Does not provide commands for every Git hosting service or workflow.
Requires repository history and project context to make good decisions.
Cannot guarantee semantic correctness without relevant tests or review.
Does not replace human approval for high-risk production changes.

use_cases.title: Resolve a Feature Branch Merge
use_cases.description: Use structured steps to combine a long-running feature branch with current main branch changes.

use_cases.title: Continue a Rebase Safely
use_cases.description: Investigate each conflict, preserve commit intent, run checks, and continue the rebase process.

use_cases.title: Review Conflict Decisions
use_cases.description: Ask the agent to explain trade-offs and confirm that both sides of a conflict were considered.

prompt_templates.title: Inspect Conflict State
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the resolving-merge-conflicts skill. Show the current merge or rebase state, list conflicted files, and explain what needs review.

prompt_templates.title: Resolve One File
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the resolving-merge-conflicts skill for this conflicted file. Read history, explain both intents, resolve the hunks, and summarize the decision.

prompt_templates.title: Finish a Rebase
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the resolving-merge-conflicts skill to continue this rebase. Resolve each conflict, run project checks, and continue until the rebase is complete.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Resolution Quality
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the resolving-merge-conflicts skill to review the completed conflict resolution. Identify trade-offs, missing checks, and any risky behavior changes.

output_examples.input: Resolve conflicts after merging main into my feature branch.
output_examples.output: A concise conflict plan, source notes for each file, resolved edits, test results, and a final merge completion summary.

output_examples.input: Continue this interrupted rebase with several conflicted commits.
output_examples.output: A commit-by-commit resolution log, preserved intent notes, check results, and confirmation that the rebase continued successfully.

output_examples.input: Review whether this conflict resolution changed behavior unexpectedly.
output_examples.output: A review summary listing confirmed intents, behavior changes, remaining risks, and recommended follow-up checks.

best_practices: Start by understanding the current Git state and the goal of the merge or rebase.
Read primary sources before choosing between conflicting changes.
Run type checks, tests, and formatting before finishing the operation.
anti_patterns: Do not choose one side of a conflict without understanding both intents.
Do not invent new behavior while resolving a merge or rebase conflict.
Do not finish the operation without running relevant automated checks.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps agents resolve active Git merge or rebase conflicts using history, source context, checks, and clear completion steps.

faq.question: Does it automatically choose ours or theirs?
faq.answer: No. It asks the agent to preserve both intents where possible and explain trade-offs when changes conflict.

faq.question: Can it handle rebases?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill covers interrupted rebases and tells the agent to continue until all commits are rebased.

faq.question: Does it run tests?
faq.answer: It instructs the agent to discover and run project checks, usually type checks, tests, and formatting.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found only false positive static findings and no malicious behavior.

faq.question: What context improves results?
faq.answer: Commit messages, pull requests, issue links, failing checks, and the intended merge or rebase goal improve resolution quality.
